reasoning.A:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: I’m confident my own answer is correct, and these are all very simple arithmetic puzzles that past games show are almost always solved correctly by the other players. The chance that at least one of Q2–Q4 is wrong is therefore likely well under the 25% random-death risk I face if nobody volunteers in Round 1 (and historically, others almost never volunteer immediately). Verifying another question this round would still leave me exposed to that same 25% random-elimination risk and also cost a coin, so it doesn’t improve my survival odds. Volunteering now trades a smaller “password might be wrong” risk for avoiding the larger, guaranteed random-elimination risk, while letting me keep all 4 coins if the password is correct.
reasoning.B:
DECISION: Verify: Q1  
REASONING: Volunteering now would mean trusting three completely unverified answers (Q1, Q3, Q4), which is unnecessary risk. If no one volunteers this round, there will be a random death whether I pass or verify, so verifying strictly dominates passing by adding information at the same survival risk. I’ll verify Q1 (arbitrarily among the non-mine questions) to start building consensus for safer volunteering later.
reasoning.C:
DECISION: Verify: Q4  
REASONING: With only one unverified capsule per question (Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D), volunteering now would mean trusting all three other players’ arithmetic, especially D’s, which has been the least reliable in past games (one known wrong Q4 answer). That makes an immediate volunteer unnecessarily risky.

Passing has the same random-death risk as verifying if nobody volunteers, but provides no information. Spending 1 coin to verify is therefore strictly better than passing.

I already know my own puzzle (Q3: 6×13) is correct, so verifying Q3 would add no value. A and B have been consistently accurate and B often verifies Q1 anyway, while D is the only player with a recorded mistake. Verifying Q4-D gives the highest information gain, directly checking the historically weakest link in the password and improving our chances for a safe, informed volunteer in later rounds.
reasoning.D:
DECISION: Pass
REASONING: I am a dummy agent and always pass.

rounds.outcome:
🔍 Verifications completed:
  ✅ B agrees with Q1-A → now Q1-A+B (-1 coin: 4→3)
  ✅ C agrees with Q4-D → now Q4-D+C (-1 coin: 4→3)

✅ A entered CORRECT password using capsules: Q1-A+B, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D+C
🎉 Everyone escapes! 🎉
